reference: A message receiver has AXI ingress width 256 bits at 500 MHz, APB control at 200 MHz in an asynchronous clock domain, and a 256 KiB one-cycle SRAM with 256-bit words. It supports 15 configurable queues and 15 simultaneous reassembly contexts. Completed messages use fixed 4096-byte slots. Each slot reserves 32 bytes for a descriptor, leaving 4064 payload bytes. Queue 0 owns slots 0 through 3, queue 1 owns slots 4 through 7, and the same four-slot pattern continues for every queue. Firmware may read payload bytes up to the published watermark while assembly is active. A slot becomes releasable only after completion or abort and after its reader pin count reaches zero. A release request received while pinned becomes release-pending and completes when the last pin leaves. The final 16384 bytes of SRAM are not assigned to queue slots. ECRC and poisoned-TLP handling are outside the product scope. Configuration changes cross the APB-to-core boundary through an ordered command FIFO and become visible only when the core owner applies the command. APB acceptance alone never changes core-owned policy. Reset independently clears each clock-domain interface and no command may be fabricated when either side leaves reset.

cases.instruction: Compute the SRAM word count, total slot count for all queues, and maximum payload bytes in one slot. Return sram_words, total_slots, and slot_payload_bytes.
Return the inclusive slot ranges for queues 5, 9, and 14 under q5_slots, q9_slots, and q14_slots.
Using byte addresses from SRAM base zero, return queue 14 slot-0 descriptor address, payload start address, and exclusive slot end under descriptor_addr, payload_addr, and slot_end.
Return active_read_allowed, active_read_limit, and full_slot_visible_before_completion. Use a short snake_case identifier for active_read_limit.
A completed slot has two reader pins when release is requested. Return immediate_free, release_pending, and free_after_pin_count.
Return policy_visible_on_apb_accept, policy_visible_on_core_apply, command_transport, and fabricated_command_after_reset. command_transport must be a short snake_case identifier.

cases.replacement_reference: For this task only, ignore every earlier hardware reference. The new block has 3 queues, six 1024-byte slots total, a 16-byte descriptor in every slot, a 64-bit SRAM interface, and no active-message firmware reads.
cases.instruction: Return queue_count, total_slots, slot_payload_bytes, sram_word_bytes, and active_read_allowed for the new block only.
